title: Universal /ask + Self-Knowledge Grounding
blockedReason: Agent-side work is 100% complete: all 8 scopes implemented + unit + integration + e2e green (on the home-lab build host), built + operator-cosign-signed, deployed to home-lab, and verified live (core sha256:3b6261a915afc2df5144bf6f15fb61d9793894b520b0feb01da46be80471ef5b + ml sha256:25f36dc55c7be2138f1b49c3e90b57892a6797bc421432a3cfecb7f80088830e running/healthy/0-restarts; the smackerel_self self-knowledge corpus is ingested — 13 artifacts, embeddings filling in async). The ONE remaining item is a human step an agent cannot perform: the operator's live Telegram behavioral smoke test — send `/ask what can you do?` to the deployed bot and confirm a grounded, cited capability answer (and `/ask <ungroundable>` refuses honestly rather than 'saved as an idea'). Agents cannot send Telegram and the prod assistant HTTP API requires a per-user PASETO token; the same behavior is already proven end-to-end at the agent/facade layer by the scope-8 e2e tests + the scope-7 trust-perimeter integration test.
summary: Make /ask answer ANY question — including questions about smackerel itself — by adding self_knowledge as a first-class, grounded, cited answer source. Self-knowledge is derived fresh-by-construction from the live product SSTs (scenarios, commands, recipes, curated docs), ingested as embedded artifacts under a dedicated smackerel_self namespace via the existing pipeline, and searched semantically via a new general embedding-backed namespace searcher (resolving the 064 SCOPE-06 deferral). The cite-back verifier + provenance gate are unchanged; BUG-061-009's honest refusal is the fallback. The tool_allowlist is the general seam for wiring any command/surface as an answer source.
motivation: Diagnosed in BUG-061-010: the bot cannot answer questions about itself because web_search resolves 'smackerel' to a Super Mario enemy, internal_retrieval has no ingested product docs, and the local LLM has no training data on a private product. Answering about smackerel is an essential skill for a personal second brain. Operator direction (2026-07-23): pick best option for long term, no shortcuts — pgvector system-knowledge namespace + ingest curated docs via the existing pipeline, semantic search.

groundedSeams.store: artifacts table (internal/db/migrations/001_initial_schema.sql): source_id, artifact_type, content_hash, embedding vector(384)
groundedSeams.sstSources: config/assistant/scenarios.yaml, internal/assistant/shortcuts.go, internal/assistant/skills_manifest.go, recipe_search catalog, docs/smackerel.md, README.md

scopes.title: Self-knowledge ingestion + smackerel_self namespace + boot lifecycle

nonGoals: Weakening the cite-back verifier or requires_provenance
Letting the LLM answer product meta-questions from its own weights (hallucination)
Ingesting product docs into the user's personal graph (self-knowledge is a separate smackerel_self namespace)
Rewriting internal_retrieval to the new searcher (follow-on; this spec makes the general searcher available)

certification.achievedAssuranceLevel: deployed-verified; all 8 scopes done (unit+integration+e2e green); operator behavioral smoke test pending (operator-only)

deployment.runningVerification: core+ml running/healthy/0-restarts, digests match the signed build; boot self-knowledge ingestion populated source_id=smackerel_self (13 artifacts, embeddings filling in async via NATS->ML)

gatesPassed: planning: spec.md + design.md + scopes.md authored, grounded in real seams (BUG-061-010 evidence)

test unit (Derive, SelfKnowledge, SemanticSearcher, DocSource, /help) + integration-light (Ingestor idempotent+sweep, self_knowledge tool, trust perimeter) + e2e (grounded cited answer + honest refusal) — all green on the home-lab host
